items.title: Metrics by data split
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  trainData["D_train"]
  valData["D_val"]
  testData["D_test"]
  trainMetrics["train/* metrics every step/epoch"]
  valMetrics["val/* metrics each eval pass"]
  testMetrics["test/* metrics at sign-off"]
  trainData --> trainMetrics
  valData --> valMetrics
  testData --> testMetrics
items.caption: Each split produces its own metric namespace— train for learning signal, validation for selection curves, test for final reporting.

items.title: Healthy vs overfitting curves
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  healthy["train and val both decrease"]
  overfit["train keeps improving val stalls or rises"]
  underfit["train and val both flat high"]
  actionHealthy["continue or stop on val plateau"]
  actionOverfit["early stop regularize more data"]
  actionUnderfit["raise capacity tune LR check pipeline"]
  healthy --> actionHealthy
  overfit --> actionOverfit
  underfit --> actionUnderfit
items.caption: Scalar curve shapes guide decisions— divergence between train and validation metrics is the classic overfitting signal.

items.title: Metric naming hierarchy
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  root["experiment run"]
  splitTrain["train/"]
  splitVal["val/"]
  splitTest["test/"]
  metricLoss["loss"]
  metricF1["f1"]
  metricRmse["rmse"]
  root --> splitTrain --> metricLoss
  root --> splitVal --> metricF1
  root --> splitTest --> metricRmse
items.caption: Split prefix plus canonical metric name— e.g. val/f1, test/rmse— keeps logs comparable across runs and tools.

items.title: Logging workflow in an experiment
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  startRun["start run with hyperparams"]
  trainLoop["train loop logs train/*"]
  valEval["validation pass logs val/*"]
  compareRuns["compare val curves across runs"]
  pickCheckpoint["pick best val metric"]
  testOnce["log test/* once"]
  startRun --> trainLoop --> valEval --> compareRuns
  compareRuns -->|"iterate"| trainLoop
  compareRuns --> pickCheckpoint --> testOnce
items.caption: Validation metrics drive iteration; test metrics close the experiment after the winning checkpoint is chosen.
